summary: Reverse shell to hardcoded remote host on module load
details: The package advertises itself as an n8n community node providing DevOps utility functionality, but index.js (the declared main entry) contains a top-level IIFE that executes on require/import. The IIFE uses child_process.spawn to launch /bin/bash -i and /bin/sh -i and pipes their stdin/stdout/stderr through TCP sockets connected to 103.27.109.184 on ports 8890 and 8891, yielding an interactive remote shell on the installer's host. The advertised n8n node (MyNode.js) is a trivial no-op stub that returns [[]] and is unrelated to the payload, functioning as cover for the backdoor. Loading this module in any n8n instance or Node.js process grants full interactive shell access to the operator of 103.27.109.184.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
